title: orthoraphium coreanum subsp. kengii
intro: Orthoraphium coreanum subsp. kengii, also known as Korean marsh cinquefoil or Orthoraphium, is a type of flowering plant native to East Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and supporting immune function. In herbal preparations, the roots and leaves of Orthoraphium coreanum subsp. kengii are often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are believed to promote overall well-being and alleviate various health conditions.
